subject: Geography
title: Treaties and Negotiations
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Treaties and Negotiations.

hook.text: The Treaty of Tordesillas in 1494 split the entire unknown world between Spain and Portugal.

buildup.text: A line drawn on a map gave Portugal Brazil and Spain everything else in the Americas.

discovery.text: Neither country had explored most of the land they claimed. They divided it sight unseen.

twist.text: The indigenous peoples living there were never consulted and never consented.

climax.text: Most of the world's borders were set by treaties between powers far from the land in question.

punchline.text: History's biggest land deals were made by people who'd never been there.

quiz.question: What did the Treaty of Tordesillas do?
options: Split the unknown world between Spain and Portugal
